mechanism_narrative: Persistently near-zero FEDFUNDS levels reduce the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and push investors out the risk curve into equities, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 returns as long as policy rates remain pinned near zero.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.14% as of 2011-03-01 vintage, consistent with ZIRP
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d SPX return falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window during the ZIRP period, or returns during near-zero rate months are not significantly above baseline (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A confirmed decline in UNRATE of at least 0.2 percentage points sustained over two consecutive monthly releases signals strengthening labor demand and improving consumer income prospects, which triggers a positive re-rating of equities over the subsequent month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by >=0.2pp over 2 consecutive monthly releases
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE revised to 9.0% as of 2011-03-01 vintage, down from cycle peak near 10%
failure_condition: In triggered instances, forward 20-day SPX returns are negative in more than 45% of cases, or the effect disappears (t-test p>0.10) when tested walk-forward across 2010-2019.

mechanism_narrative: Joint improvement in RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) indicates simultaneous strengthening of consumer spending and residential investment, two independent demand channels whose co-movement signals a durable broadening of aggregate demand that supports equity valuations over a quarterly horizon beyond what either series predicts alone.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS revised value 370775 vs vintage 389320 showing volatility, HOUST at 517k revised from 479k
failure_condition: The joint RSAFS+HOUST co-movement signal produces forward 60-day returns statistically indistinguishable from the univariate RSAFS-only or HOUST-only signal (interaction term coefficient not significant at 10% level in walk-forward regression).

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery regime, month-over-month increases in INDPRO reflect resumption of industrial capacity utilization and corporate earnings momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns; this relationship is specific to early-recovery regimes and weakens or reverses in late-cycle or overheating regimes.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 94.08 revised value as of 2011-03-01, still below pre-crisis peak indicating ongoing recovery
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO-return relationship fails to hold (Rank IC < 0.03) when tested specifically within the 2010-2012 recovery window, or the same signal produces negative returns in more than 40% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation rises above 3% while FEDFUNDS remains pinned below 0.5%, the combination signals a policy trap where the Fed cannot respond to rising inflation without derailing recovery, raising stagflation risk and compressing equity risk premia, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL revised 223.046 with rising commodity-driven inflation pressure in early 2011, FEDFUNDS at 0.14%
failure_condition: When the joint trigger fires, forward 60-day SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction effect is not statistically distinguishable from the CPIAUCSL-only effect (interaction coefficient p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Monthly increases in PAYEMS (nonfarm payrolls) provide the fastest-moving, most closely watched labor signal to markets, producing an immediate positive short-horizon equity reaction as payroll surprises are quickly priced into risk assets within the following trading week.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS revised to 131283 thousand from vintage 130738 as of 2011-03-01, showing continued payroll growth
failure_condition: Rank IC between monthly PAYEMS change and forward 5-day SPX return is below 0.02 over any rolling 1-year window, or reaction is not concentrated around payroll release dates.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST below 500 thousand annualized units signals renewed weakness in residential construction demand, historically a leading indicator of broader economic slowdown, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month as markets price in reduced growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 500 thousand annualized units
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST revised to 517k, close to threshold, as of 2011-02-01 vintage
failure_condition: When HOUST is below 500k, forward 20-day SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime where GDPC1 growth is decelerating alongside a still-elevated UNRATE, weak aggregate output combined with slack labor markets signals a fragile recovery that amplifies downside sensitivity to any negative supply-side shock in INDPRO, producing negative equity returns specifically during fragile-recovery regimes but not during robust expansions.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.0% while GDPC1 vintage shows below-trend growth as of 2010-10-01
regime_hint: fragile post-crisis recovery with elevated unemployment
failure_condition: The joint GDPC1/UNRATE/INDPRO fragility signal fails to produce negative forward 60-day returns more often than the unconditional base rate (34% negative days) when tested walk-forward across 2010-2013.
